data.title: Tomra Systems Q4 EBITA Adjusted EUR 71 Mln
data.description:
Tomra Systems ASA :

  • TOMRA SYSTEMS Q4 GROSS MARGIN 46%

  • Q4 REVENUES WERE 382 MEUR (4Q24: 398 MEUR) IN TOMRA GROUP, DOWN 4%

  • Q4 EBITA (ADJUSTED. FOR SPECIAL ITEMS) WAS 71 MEUR (4Q24: 78 MEUR)

  • Q4 TOMRA SYSTEMS EPS (ADJUSTED. FOR SPECIAL ITEMS) WAS 0.15 EUR (4Q24: 0.18 EUR)

  • PROPOSED DIVIDEND NOK 2.15 PER SHARE (NOK 2.15), CORRESPONDING TO A PAYOUT RATIO OF 58% OF EPS

  • TOTAL CAPITAL EXPENDITURES OF APPROXIMATELY 100 MEUR ARE EXPECTED IN 2026, PRIMARILY DIRECTED TOWARDS PROJECTS IN CORE DIVISIONS COLLECTION, RECYCLING, AND FOOD

  • CURRENT ORDERS IN POLAND, PORTUGAL, AND SINGAPORE ARE EXPECTED TO CONTRIBUTE WITH APPROXIMATELY 100 MEUR IN REVENUES IN 2026

data.title: India's SpiceJet falls on posting quarterly loss
data.description:
** Shares of SpiceJet down 2.7% to 19.87 rupees, set to fall for third straight session

** Airline posts loss of 2.69 bln rupees ($29.7 mln) in Q3 vs profit of 249.7 mln rupees year ago

** Q3 total revenue up 12.4% Y/Y at 13.84 bln rupees

** SPJT fell 46% in 2025

($1 = 90.7370 Indian rupees)

data.title: Norway's Tomra Systems Q4 revenue falls 4% yr/yr
data.description:
Overview

  • Norway-based sensor solutions firm's Q4 revenue fell 4% yr/yr

  • Adjusted EPS for Q4 was 0.15 EUR, down from 0.18 EUR last year

  • Company announces cost reduction program to improve profitability

Outlook

  • Tomra Systems to operate Singapore's deposit system starting April 2026

  • Food order backlog grows 26% with large orders for 2026

Result Drivers

  • COLLECTION GROWTH - Record high revenues in Collection driven by installations in Poland and Portugal, with more installations expected

  • RECYCLING CHALLENGES - Recycling revenues down 27% yr/yr, reflecting challenging market conditions

  • FOOD SEGMENT RESILIENCE - Food segment profitability exceeded restructuring targets with a 26% growth in order backlog

Key Details

Metric

Q4 Gross Margin

Beat/Miss

Actual

46.00%

Consensus Estimate

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 10 "strong buy" or "buy", 1 "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the industrial machinery & equipment peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Tomra Systems ASA is NOK153.00, about 14.2% above its February 12 closing price of NOK134.00

  • The stock recently traded at 27 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 22 three months ago

Press Release:

data.title: Sweden's Fingerprint Cards Q4 revenue hit by FX, swings to positive EBITDA
data.description:
Overview

  • Biometrics leader's Q4 revenue decreased 4% yr/yr due to currency effects

  • Q4 EBITDA improved to positive SEK 0.4 M from negative SEK 27.6 M

  • Company launched FPC AllKey Ultra, expanding biometric product lineup

Outlook

  • Fingerprint Cards plans mass production of AllKey systems in the second half of 2026

  • Company aims to monetize assets and drive growth through AI augmentation

  • Fingerprint Cards sees non-organic growth as a potential lever in 2026

Result Drivers

  • ALLKEY TRACTION - Co reported strong customer feedback and adoption metrics for AllKey, highlighting its ease of integration and performance

  • ASSET MONETIZATION - Licensing agreements with Smart Eye, Egis Technology, and PixArt contributed to improved earnings and cash flow

  • ALLKEY ULTRA LAUNCH - Co launched AllKey Ultra, expanding into high-security use cases with a secure element

data.title: Norway's AF Gruppen Q4 revenue rises on Civil Engineering unit strength
data.description:

  • Norway construction firm's Q4 revenue rose

  • Q4 pretax profit increased

  • Company completed acquisition of majority shares in Brødrene Myhre

Outlook

  • AF Gruppen plans to strengthen project management and expertise to enhance competitiveness

  • Company seeks new growth opportunities through acquisitions

Result Drivers

  • CIVIL ENGINEERING GROWTH - Revenue increased by 12% due to high activity and efficient operations in major projects

  • ENERGY AND ENVIRONMENT EXPANSION - Revenue growth of 48% driven by acquisition of AF Elkraft, leading to strong profitability
